name: The Flood
title: The crew you never have to feed.
body: Open one gate and the whole line wakes — follow-up, scheduling, the busywork, striking in sequence. Built once; it runs every night without you.
entry_state: warm amber light spilling from behind the camera into a vast dark hall
exit_state: the vast hall half-alight — molten channels flowing, hammers striking — with the camera drifting toward a lit runner channel leaving the hall
still_169: Cinematic photorealistic night world of an ancient Celtic iron forge carved into black bedrock. Near-total darkness — the only light: a racing flood of molten metal lighting half the hall's stations while the rest lie dark. Deep near-black shadows (#0A0908); where lit, molten amber highlights (#E9A94F), ember orange (#E2622B), rust (#B5623A), warm cream glow (#E7CFAE); where dead, cold ash grey-blue on black iron. Volumetric smoke haze, wet black stone with faint reflections, subtle carved Celtic knotwork worked into the rock surfaces. Dark industrial realism, low-key chiaroscuro lighting, anamorphic cinematic feel, atmospheric depth. No people, no figures, no hands, no text, no letters, no numbers, no signage, no logos. Subject: wide forge hall mid-flood — half the stations molten and striking, trip hammers mid-strike over glowing iron with spark showers and crucibles tipping, half the hall still cold and dark, the glowing flood front of molten metal visible in the stone runner channel where lit stations give way to dead ones.

Vertical 9:16 composition, tall frame, key subject centered, generous dark headroom above for overlay copy.
leg_prompt: The camera continues the same slow, steady forward drift it was already in, without any pause, jump, or reversal. Cinematic photorealistic night world of an ancient Celtic iron forge carved into black bedrock. Near-total darkness — the only light: warm amber light spilling from behind the camera into a vast dark hall. Deep near-black shadows (#0A0908); where lit, molten amber highlights (#E9A94F), ember orange (#E2622B), rust (#B5623A), warm cream glow (#E7CFAE); where dead, cold ash grey-blue on black iron. Volumetric smoke haze, wet black stone with faint reflections, subtle carved Celtic knotwork worked into the rock surfaces. Dark industrial realism, low-key chiaroscuro lighting, anamorphic cinematic feel, atmospheric depth. No people, no figures, no hands, no text, no letters, no numbers, no signage, no logos. The camera emerges from the passage into the vast dark hall as a flood of molten metal surges down the stone runner channel ahead of it, racing forward and lighting station after station in sequence: iron gates along the channel swing open on their own, trip hammers wake one after another and begin striking glowing iron with returning showers of sparks, and great crucibles begin to tip molten metal toward rows of dark molds. Never slowing, the camera drifts down low and tracks slowly alongside the waking production line, close to the striking hammers and flowing metal, sparks drifting past the lens, the hall now half-alight behind it — molten channels flowing, hammers striking — as it drifts steadily toward a lit runner channel leaving the hall at the far side. Single continuous camera move with no cuts. Smooth, graceful, slow cinematic motion. No people, no text. In the final second the camera settles into a slow, steady forward drift, perfectly smooth.
